howItWorks.intro: This golden hour duration calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Duration
rules.explanation: Higher latitudes have longer golden hours, especially in winter.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: When is golden hour?
faq.answer: The hour after sunrise and before sunset when light is warm and soft.

faq.question: Why is golden hour better for photos?
faq.answer: Low sun angle creates warm colors, long shadows, and diffused light.

faq.question: Is the Golden Hour Duration Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What latitude should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate latitude value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Golden Hour Duration Calculator?
sections.body: The Golden Hour Duration Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your latitude, month, you get instant results including golden hour duration, light quality. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting golden hour duration right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your latitude and need to find the right golden hour duration. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Latitude (degrees)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your latitude in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Month
howToSteps.description: Type or select your month in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your golden hour duration, light quality. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your latitude before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
